intent.oneSentenceIntent: A simple, fast macOS calculator for everyday arithmetic.
targetUsers: a Mac user who wants quick everyday arithmetic without a heavy app
intent.problem: reaching for a calculator should be instant and never surprising
intent.desiredOutcome: type or click a calculation and get the correct result immediately

coreJourneys.actor: a Mac user doing quick math
steps: type or click the second number
coreJourneys.expectedOutcome: the correct result is shown immediately
coreJourneys.failureRecovery: dividing by zero shows "Error" instead of crashing; Clear (or Esc) resets to 0 to continue

sources.title: user asked for a simple calculator
macOS Calculator (system app) — the platform-standard calculator behavior

analogClaims.claim: everyday calculators provide the four basic operations, a clear/reset, and accept keyboard input, and never crash on bad input

baseline.statement: perform the four basic operations (+ - x /) and show the correct result on =
handle division by zero gracefully — show an error and never crash

customDelta.statement: support a decimal point for non-integer numbers
accept keyboard input (digits, operators, Return, Esc)

successCriteria: arithmetic results are correct for the four basic operations
dividing by zero shows an error and the app keeps working

then: the app does not crash and can be cleared to continue

decisionLog.decision: no persistence — calculations are transient, in-memory only
decisionLog.rationale: a simple calculator has no reason to store user data; keeps it offline and private

decisionLog.decision: default to a basic four-function calculator
decisionLog.rationale: matches the explicit request and the platform standard; scientific/history are non-goals

buildMustPreserve: the four basic operations are correct
division by zero is safe (no crash)
stopConditions: any requirement implies a network call
any requirement implies persisting user data
